what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note in which Tim replies to an earlier paragraph about six existing scopes, asking for more explanation of what is wrong and what is proposed for dragnet outputs, while affirming the concept of resolution and attachment.
observations: YAML front matter declares id item-0c9af73f, board:// address, type note, source claude_code, state posted, channel dragnet-development, author_session tim
A links entry of kind commented_on points to board://item-9645a809
Created and updated timestamps are identical and a history entry records a filed transition from null to posted
Body begins with a [paragraph] tag and quotes a prior statement about six scopes: channel, session, project-of-the-channel, global, working-dir, and heaps
Tim responds that more explanation is needed for dragnet outputs and channels, and highlights the word attachment as a key concept tied to a previous comment
standouts: It is a reply/comment on item-9645a809, not a top-level post
The central signal is the insistence on attachment as the relevant relation rather than simple scope inclusion
It asks for clarification about what is wrong and what is proposed specifically for dragnet outputs
concerns: The phrase 'as what you mean by wrong' is grammatically awkward and may obscure the exact request

notes: A design-discussion reply by Tim that accepts the scope-ladder idea but pushes for clearer dragnet-output semantics and an attachment model.
questions: What specific proposal about dragnet outputs is being responded to?
What does 'wrong' refer to in the prior paragraph?

purpose_vs_actual: The title 'Tim · paragraph' only labels the author and format; the actual content is a reply asking for clarification and affirming an attachment concept.
apparent_themes: extending an existing mechanism to new outputs
design rationale in a channel-based ledger
intention_signals: Tim seems to be trying to align the discussion on a shared mechanism by validating the ladder of scopes while demanding that dragnet outputs be explained in terms of attachment rather than just scope placement.
contribution: It preserves a clarification request and the 'attachment' framing for how dragnet outputs should resolve to context, plus a link to the prior comment it responds to.
summary_for_embedding: A posted noticeboard reply by Tim in dragnet-development commenting on item-9645a809, affirming a six-scope ladder concept but requesting clearer explanation of dragnet output semantics and emphasizing attachment.
intention_for_embedding: To refine a design proposal by asking for more detail on what is wrong and what attachment-based resolution should look like for dragnet outputs.
